---
title: "Question2"
---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
library(doParallel)
library(foreach)
library(MASS)
data("galaxies")
```

We use `doParallel` and `foreach` to bootstrap the median for the galaxies data in the MASS library with the purpose of comparing times between the two approaches.

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Set up cluster
cl <- makeCluster(4)
registerDoParallel(cl)

B <- 1000  # Number of bootstrap samples

# METHOD 1: PARALLEL
time_individual <- system.time({
  res_parallel <- foreach(i = 1:B, 
                         .combine = c, 
                         .packages = "MASS",
                         .export = "galaxies") %dopar% {
    median(sample(galaxies, replace = TRUE))
  }
})
cat("Time elapsed:", time_individual["elapsed"], "seconds\n")
cat("First few results:", head(res_parallel), "\n\n")

# METHOD 2: PARALLEL BUT CHUNKED
chunk_size <- 100
num_chunks <- B / chunk_size

time_chunked <- system.time({
  res_chunked <- foreach(chunk = 1:num_chunks, 
                        .combine = c,
                        .packages = "MASS",
                        .export = "galaxies") %dopar% {
    
    # Process chunk_size bootstrap samples in ONE worker
    chunk_results <- replicate(chunk_size, {
      median(sample(galaxies, replace = TRUE))
    })
    
    return(chunk_results)
  }
})
cat("Time elapsed:", time_chunked["elapsed"], "seconds\n")
cat("First few results:", head(res_chunked), "\n\n")

# METHOD 3: SERIAL (BASELINE)
time_serial <- system.time({
  res_serial <- foreach(i = 1:B, 
                       .combine = c, 
                       .packages = "MASS") %do% {
    median(sample(galaxies, replace = TRUE))
  }
})
cat("Time elapsed:", time_serial["elapsed"], "seconds\n")
cat("First few results:", head(res_serial), "\n\n")

# Stop the cluster
stopCluster(cl)

# Create comparison table
comparison <- data.frame(
  Method = c("Parallel (individual)", "Parallel (chunked)", "Serial (baseline)"),
  Time_sec = c(time_individual["elapsed"], 
               time_chunked["elapsed"], 
               time_serial["elapsed"])
)

# Calculate speedup relative to serial
comparison$Speedup_vs_serial <- round(comparison$Time_sec[3] / comparison$Time_sec, 2)
comparison$Efficiency <- round(comparison$Speedup_vs_serial / 4 * 100, 1)  # 4 cores

print(comparison)


```
